pages.title: Surgeries — Callum Anderson for Buckingham & Bletchley
headings: h3: What are constituency surgeries? And how can I help?
pages.text_excerpt: Surgeries — Callum Anderson for Buckingham & Bletchley 0 Skip to Content About me Introduction How can I help you? My role as your MP Constituency Surgeries Casework Parliament Visits Office Standards Accountable to you My Priorities My Visits Annual Report Bletchley Investment Taskforce Work for me Contact me News and views Open Menu Close Menu About me Introduction How can I help you? My role as your MP Constituency Surgeries Casework Parliament Visits Office Standards Accountable to you My Priorities My Visits Annual Report Bletchley Investment Taskforce Work for me Contact me News and views Open Menu Close Menu Folder: About me Back Introduction Folder: How can I help you? Back My role as your MP Constituency Surgeries Casework Parliament Visits Office Standards Folder: Accountable to you Back My Priorities My Visits Annual Report Bletchley Investment Taskforce Work for me Contact me News and views Upcoming constituency surgeries Below are the dates of my upcoming constituency surgeries. Email my team to make an appointment and find out the exact location - and please note that surgeries have to be booked at least 24 hours ahead of time. Bletchley: Friday 5 June, 12:30-13:30 Winslow: Friday 12 June, 11:30-12:30 Buckingham: Friday 19 June, 15:00-16:00 Bletchley: Friday 26 June, 17:00-18:30 What are constituency surgeries? And how can I help? Constituency surgeries provide people living within the towns, villages and city that make up the Buckingham and Bletchley constituency the opportunity to raise issues of a personal or national nature with me directly. I can offer assistance with matters that Parliament and central government are responsible for. This will include government departments (e.g. Department for Work and Pensions, or the Home Office). I can also write to senior officials or Ministers in government departments and agencies. My team and I will try to help in any way that we can, but some issues fall outside of the remit of a Member of Parliament. If your case relates to matters which either Milton Keynes City Council or Buckinghamshire Council are responsible for I encourage you to seek support from your elected ward councillors in the first instance – this will include housing, parking, waste collection and planning. I’m afraid that I cannot help you fill an application form for benefits or housing, or with legal problems. I also cannot write to private individuals (for example, your neighbours) on your behalf. Strict parliamentary protocol dictates that MPs can only assist their own constituents . Please check that your postcode falls within the Buckingham and Bletchley constituency before coming along . You can do so here. Here are some other useful telephone numbers: Emergency services (urgent) – 999 Medical help (non-urgent) – 111 Police (non-urgent) – 101 National Domestic Abuse Helpline (Refuge) – 0808 2000 247 Samaritans – 116 123 Citizens Advice – 0344 477 0808 Street Link (National Homeless Support Helpline) – 0300 500 0914 Shelter - 0808 800 4444. Sign up to my newsletter here Privacy Page ⓒ Callum Anderson 2024

My role as your MP Constituency Surgeries Casework Parliament Visits Office Standards Accountable to you My Priorities My Visits Annual Report Bletchley Investment Taskforce Work for me Contact me News and views Open Menu Close Menu Folder: About me Back Introduction Folder: How can I help you? Back My role as your MP Constituency Surgeries Casework Parliament Visits Office Standards Folder: Accountable to you Back My Priorities My Visits Annual Report Bletchley Investment Taskforce Work for me Contact me News and views Standing up for your priorities Before and after the election, I have been speaking to voters, trade unionists, farmers, businesses, and civil society leaders from across Buckingham, Bletchley, Tattenhoe, Winslow and the surrounding villages to understand people’s concerns. People have told me they want a fresh start, and I have listened. As your Labour MP, I’ll stand up for your priorities to fresh hope, fresh opportunities and importantly, a fresh start. Boost household incomes Wages flatlined under the Conservatives. As someone with financial services experience, I recognise the importance of enabling local businesses to access finance for growth, innovation, and job creation. I'm also a dedicated trade unionist, who strongly believes in the need for responsible business practices, such as proper living wages and decent working conditions. The incoming East-West Rail line will place Winslow, Buckingham and Bletchley right at the heart of the Oxford-Cambridge arc: an area which has the potential to become a world-leading and globally renowned centre for business. I will work tirelessly to make sure families in our towns and villages see the benefits and get the opportunities those links will bring. As your MP, I will: · Fight for stronger workers’ rights - including banning fire and rehire, establishing Fair Pay Agreements and extending statutory maternity and paternity leave. · Set up an Investment Taskforce , bringing together local employers, the South Central Institute of Technology and civil society. This Taskforce will aim to to attract the new businesses, jobs and apprenticeships so that Bletchley can fully realise the promise of its Town Deal. · Campaign for business rates reform , so that we properly support the small businesses, village pubs and independent shops that give life to Buckingham High Street, Winslow High Street, Bletchley’s Queensway, Fenny Stratford High Street and our villages. Tackle the cost of living The Tory cost of living crisis has affected working people across Buckingham and Bletchley. I've regularly come across families struggling to meet the soaring costs of housing, energy and food. Our Labour Government will end the cycle of higher prices, higher interest rates and higher taxes - and will put money back into the pockets of hardworking people and their families. As your MP, I will: · Champion an industrial strategy that delivers investment in renewable energy sources like solar, wind and biomass, so that we keep energy bills low for good. · Campaign for planning reform and the cash that councils need to build more homes — so that every family has the security of a genuinely affordable and environmentally sustainable home. · Convene local farmers, food manufacturers and retailers to agree measures that tackle local food insecurity and reduce the weekly food bill for families. Repair our local NHS Getting a GP or dental appointment has never been harder. Waiting lists remain far too long. The Buckinghamshire Conservative Council is still dragging its heels over Buckingham’s medical c

pages.title: Work for me — Callum Anderson for Buckingham & Bletchley
headings: h3: Constituency Assistant
pages.text_excerpt: Work for me — Callum Anderson for Buckingham & Bletchley 0 Skip to Content About me Introduction How can I help you? My role as your MP Constituency Surgeries Casework Parliament Visits Office Standards Accountable to you My Priorities My Visits Annual Report Bletchley Investment Taskforce Work for me Contact me News and views Open Menu Close Menu About me Introduction How can I help you? My role as your MP Constituency Surgeries Casework Parliament Visits Office Standards Accountable to you My Priorities My Visits Annual Report Bletchley Investment Taskforce Work for me Contact me News and views Open Menu Close Menu Folder: About me Back Introduction Folder: How can I help you? Back My role as your MP Constituency Surgeries Casework Parliament Visits Office Standards Folder: Accountable to you Back My Priorities My Visits Annual Report Bletchley Investment Taskforce Work for me Contact me News and views Constituency Assistant Part-time (four days per week), £22,400 per year (equivalent to £28,000 full-time) Office-based in Milton Keynes/North Buckinghamshire Further part-time/flexible working considered Post overview In this role, you will play a pivotal part in ensuring the smooth and effective operation of Callum’s Buckingham and Bletchley constituency office. Working within a small, committed team, you will contribute to the full breadth of constituency work - from office administration and casework support to accompanying Callum at local events. This is a dynamic and hugely rewarding position, well suited to someone who thrives in a busy environment. It calls for empathy, sound judgement and excellent organisational skills, alongside the ability to manage a diverse workload with efficiency, accuracy and discretion. Key responsibilities Diary management and organisation of constituency meetings and events. Assisting at surgeries and other meetings - following up as appropriate. Attending constituency meetings and events. Supporting management of local projects and campaigns. Supporting day-to-day casework. This includes logging cases, monitoring progress and ensuring all identified actions are taken. Ensuring all enquiries are managed with sensitivity and confidentiality, and responded to promptly. Gathering relevant information from external agencies to assist with resolving cases. Drafting professional and accurate responses to constituents. Ensuring records are kept and information are managed confidentially in line with data protection legislation (GDPR). The post holder should be ready to undertake other duties as required and prepared to work outside of the normal working hours, including occasional travel and attending events with Callum. Desired attributes, skills, and experience Essential skills and experience: Diary management and organising meetings and events. Direct public facing customer service or frequent public engagement. The ability to communicate effectively in writing. Excellent organisation, planning, and prioritisation capabilities. Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills. Proven capability to work collaboratively within a team. Desirable Skills: Experience of responding to casework a bonus. Personal attributes: Calm, polite, confident, and empathetic when engaging with the public. A high level of discretion and confidentiality. A public service ethos – with a strong commitment to helping Callum deliver on his aspiration to be a proactive, accessible, and visible Member of Parliament. Willingness to get involved across the breadth of Callum’s and the team’s work. Driven to improve the lives of people across the Buckingham and Bletchley constituency and the UK more broadly. Sympathetic with the aims and values of the Labour Party. How to apply To apply, send a cover letter detailing your motivations for applying for this role and your CV outlining your career experience, achievements, and skills to thomas.welch@parliament.uk by 8pm, Tuesday 5 May. We reserve the right to close the applicati

pages.title: Introduction — Callum Anderson for Buckingham & Bletchley
pages.text_excerpt: Introduction — Callum Anderson for Buckingham & Bletchley 0 Skip to Content About me Introduction How can I help you? My role as your MP Constituency Surgeries Casework Parliament Visits Office Standards Accountable to you My Priorities My Visits Annual Report Bletchley Investment Taskforce Work for me Contact me News and views Open Menu Close Menu About me Introduction How can I help you? My role as your MP Constituency Surgeries Casework Parliament Visits Office Standards Accountable to you My Priorities My Visits Annual Report Bletchley Investment Taskforce Work for me Contact me News and views Open Menu Close Menu Folder: About me Back Introduction Folder: How can I help you? Back My role as your MP Constituency Surgeries Casework Parliament Visits Office Standards Folder: Accountable to you Back My Priorities My Visits Annual Report Bletchley Investment Taskforce Work for me Contact me News and views Hi there, I'm Callum. I am privileged to serve as the Labour Member of Parliament for the Buckingham and Bletchley constituency. Below is a little more about me… Local roots I was born in Luton and grew up in nearby Dunstable. My mum brought me up alone in a council flat, while she worked shifts in Morrisons. I went to local comprehensive schools and depended on local public services . Getting by on a low income, life wasn’t always easy. I watched my mum painstakingly manage her monthly wages to pay the bills, put food on the table and give me as many opportunities as she could. Through her example, she taught me the values of hard work, perseverance, and to aspire for a better life for myself and my community. Labour Governments transform lives — I know because it transformed mine But I was lucky. My childhood coincided with the last Labour Government. It was thanks to that Government which invested in families like mine — through establishing the minimum wage, introducing tax credits and record investment in our local state schools — that I was part of a Labour legacy which lifted a million children out of poverty and enabled me to be the first in my family to go to university . That commitment to tackling inequality, giving dignity to work and making a first-class education a right for every child is what inspired me to join the Labour Party at the age of 18. The Conservatives failed Buckingham and Bletchley Fast forward to the brink of the 2024 general election, and Britain was back to square one. The Tories failed ordinary working people in the Buckingham and Bletchley area repeatedly over 14 years in Government. Following the election, the Labour Government has inherited a country where the cost of living is on the rise, yet wages are standing still. More people than ever are in insecure work and it is clear that our public services are at breaking point. Council flat kids like me in places like Buckingham, Bletchley, Winslow and Tattenhoe have never faced a steeper mountain to climb. Tory incompetence has robbed every parent and family of a simple wish: to give their child things they were never able to have. We now have a change of Government and a chance to change things. I will work hard every day as your Member of Parliament to provide more opportunities so that every individual, family and community can live a better life. It’s time for a fresh start Families in Buckingham and Bletchley deserve better. Our Labour Government is on your side. We are committed to spreading opportunity to every part of the country so that everyone has the chance to get a job that pays a decent wage, the ability to send their children to a great local school and the possibility of owning an affordable home. I’m ready to go On the lead-up to the election, I listened to residents, businesses, faith groups and all kinds of civil society organisations to get their views about how we do things differently in the Buckingham and Bletchley constituency, and get the best for our communities. I want to keep these conversations going, so th

pages.title: My role as your MP — Callum Anderson for Buckingham & Bletchley
pages.text_excerpt: My role as your MP — Callum Anderson for Buckingham & Bletchley 0 Skip to Content About me Introduction How can I help you? My role as your MP Constituency Surgeries Casework Parliament Visits Office Standards Accountable to you My Priorities My Visits Annual Report Bletchley Investment Taskforce Work for me Contact me News and views Open Menu Close Menu About me Introduction How can I help you? My role as your MP Constituency Surgeries Casework Parliament Visits Office Standards Accountable to you My Priorities My Visits Annual Report Bletchley Investment Taskforce Work for me Contact me News and views Open Menu Close Menu Folder: About me Back Introduction Folder: How can I help you? Back My role as your MP Constituency Surgeries Casework Parliament Visits Office Standards Folder: Accountable to you Back My Priorities My Visits Annual Report Bletchley Investment Taskforce Work for me Contact me News and views My role as your MP As Buckingham and Bletchley’s MP, I will act as your voice in Parliament, and you can ask me questions about my votes in the House of Commons; as well as my stance on issues that matter to you. I promise that I take this part of the job incredibly seriously. I will always vote with my conscience and for what I believe to be the best interest of my constituents. As your representative, I am accountable to you, and I encourage local residents to get in touch to tell me about their issues and problems. However, with such a busy inbox, I do have to prioritise messages asking me specific questions and regarding urgent casework ahead of campaign emails, but I will always get back to you. If you are contacting me from an organisation located within the constituency we will aim to respond as soon as possible. If you are located outside Buckingham and Bletchley, a response will be provided on a discretionary basis. I aim to be a proactive, visible, and accessible MP, and this is why I place such importance of getting out and about and going to every part of the patch. I’ve already visited dozens of schools, charities, and independent businesses and love to highlight how brilliant and diverse our patch is. If you would like me to stop by, do let me know. Please also bear in mind that my diary is usually planned at least two months ahead. Sign up to my newsletter here Privacy Page ⓒ Callum Anderson 2024
